title: Simulating Percolation in Python: How Do Wildfires & Diseases Spread?
summary: This video tutorial demonstrates how to implement percolation simulations in Python to model the spread of wildfires and diseases. Percolation theory is used to understand how phenomena propagate through networks and systems.
writeup: This NeuralNine tutorial implements percolation simulations in Python to model how wildfires and diseases spread. It frames percolation theory as a way to understand how phenomena propagate through networks and systems. The takeaway is a working Python implementation that connects the abstract theory to concrete spreading dynamics across a grid or network.
